title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat by exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, potentially leading to widespread data encryption, service outages, and financial losses for organizations relying on cloud services.
recommended_actions: Review and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing strict access controls, multi-factor authentication, and regular security audits.
Deploy advanced ransomware detection and prevention tools across cloud environments to identify and mitigate threats early.
Ensure regular backups of critical data are performed, stored offline, and tested for restoration to minimize downtime in case of an attack.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal direct risk, but improper implementation or over-reliance on automated tools without manual validation could lead to missed vulnerabilities or false positives. Additionally, if the tool is compromised, it could introduce malicious code into scanned systems.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code and community trustworthiness before integrating it into your workflow.
Use the tool as a supplementary measure alongside manual code reviews and other security practices.
Monitor the tool's updates and ensure it is downloaded from a verified source to avoid supply chain attacks.

title: In nltk/nltk versions 3.9.3 and earlier, five Stanford interface classes (StanfordPOSTagger,...
description: In nltk/nltk versions 3.9.3 and earlier, five Stanford interface classes (StanfordPOSTagger, StanfordNERTagger, StanfordParser, StanfordDependencyParser, and StanfordNeuralDependencyParser) are vulnerable to untrusted JAR code execution. These classes accept user-controllable JAR paths and execute them via the `java()` function, which invokes `subprocess.Popen()` without integrity verification. This vulnerability is identical to CVE-2026-0848, which was fixed for StanfordSegmenter by adding SHA2

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-12252.

title: picklescan before 0.0.28 fails to detect malicious pickle files that use torch.utils.data...
description: picklescan before 0.0.28 fails to detect malicious pickle files that use torch.utils.data.datapipes.utils.decoder.basichandlers in reduce methods, allowing attackers to bypass safety checks. Remote attackers can embed undetected malicious code in pickle files that executes during deserialization, enabling remote code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-71369.

title: picklescan before 0.0.34 fails to detect the _operator.methodcaller built-in function when...
description: picklescan before 0.0.34 fails to detect the _operator.methodcaller built-in function when scanning pickle files for malicious code. Attackers can craft malicious pickle payloads using _operator.methodcaller that evade detection and execute arbitrary code when loaded by pickle.load().

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-71375.

title: Picklescan before 0.0.33 fails to detect the numpy.f2py.crackfortran.getlincoef gadget in pickle...
description: Picklescan before 0.0.33 fails to detect the numpy.f2py.crackfortran.getlincoef gadget in pickle __reduce__ methods, allowing arbitrary code execution. Attackers can craft malicious pickle files that execute arbitrary Python code when loaded, bypassing Picklescan's safety checks and enabling supply-chain poisoning of shared model files.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Picklescan before 0.0.33 allows attackers to execute arbitrary Python code via malicious pickle files, potentially compromising systems that rely on pickle file deserialization. This can lead to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or supply-chain attacks, particularly in environments where shared model files are used.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Picklescan to version 0.0.33 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement strict validation and sandboxing for all deserialized pickle files to prevent execution of malicious code.
Educate developers and system administrators about the risks of deserializing untrusted pickle files and encourage the use of safer serialization formats where possible.

title: picklescan before 0.0.33 fails to detect operator.methodcaller function calls in pickle files,...
description: picklescan before 0.0.33 fails to detect operator.methodcaller function calls in pickle files, allowing attackers to bypass security checks. Remote attackers can craft malicious pickle payloads using operator.methodcaller that execute arbitrary code when loaded, compromising systems relying on picklescan for validation.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-71373.

title: The Execute Command node in n8n allows authenticated users to execute arbitrary commands on the...
description: The Execute Command node in n8n allows authenticated users to execute arbitrary commands on the host system where n8n runs. Attackers with user access or compromised credentials can exploit this node to run malicious commands, potentially leading to data exfiltration, service disruption, or complete system compromise.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Execute Command node of n8n allows authenticated users to execute arbitrary commands on the host system. This could lead to data exfiltration, service disruption, or full system compromise, especially if attackers gain access to sensitive credentials or privileged accounts.
recommended_actions: Immediately update n8n to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2025-71380.
Restrict access to n8n instances by implementing network-level access controls and ensuring strong, unique passwords for all user accounts.
Monitor logs for unusual activity, such as unauthorized command execution, and revoke compromised credentials immediately.

title: An Incorrect Use of Privileged APIs vulnerability in Unity Parsec on Windows hosts leads to a...
description: An Incorrect Use of Privileged APIs vulnerability in Unity Parsec on Windows hosts leads to a potential Elevation of Privilege. This issue affects Parsec through v2026-05-04.0. The patched version is Parsec for Windows version 150-104a. A user can generate a situation where there is an instance of parsecd.exe running as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM with a user-controlled value of the AppData environment variable.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-54424.

Attackers can craft malicious pickle files embedding this built-in function that evade detection but execute arbitrary commands when loaded.

description: picklescan before 0.0.29 fails to detect malicious pickle files using idlelib.calltip.get_entity function in reduce methods. Attackers can embed undetected code in pickle files that executes remote commands when loaded by victims.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in picklescan before version 0.0.29 allows attackers to embed malicious code in pickle files, which can execute remote commands when loaded. This poses a significant risk of unauthorized code execution, data breaches, and potential system compromise.
recommended_actions: Upgrade picklescan to version 0.0.29 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement strict validation and sanitization of pickle files before loading them, especially from untrusted sources.
Educate developers and system administrators about the risks of using pickle files and encourage the use of safer serialization formats where possible.

description: picklescan before 0.0.30 fails to detect malicious pickle files using idlelib.run.Executive.runcode in reduce methods. Attackers can embed undetected code in pickle files that executes during pickle.load, enabling remote code execution in PyTorch models and supply chain attacks.

title: Improper access control in Microsoft Edge for Android allows an unauthorized attacker to bypass a...
description: Improper access control in Microsoft Edge for Android allows an unauthorized attacker to bypass a security feature over a network.

title: CVE-2026-11773 - The Masteriyo LMS – LMS Course Builder, Quizzes & Certificates plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to
description: The Masteriyo LMS – LMS Course Builder, Quizzes & Certificates plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all versions up to, and including, 2.2.1. This is due to the plugin not properly verifying that a user is authorized to perform an action. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with student-level access and above, to modify the description (post content) of arbitrary course announcements authored by instructors or administrators.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users with student-level access or higher to modify course announcement content, potentially leading to misinformation, unauthorized changes, or reputational damage for organizations using the plugin.
recommended_actions: Update the Masteriyo LMS plugin to the latest patched version once available.
Restrict user roles and permissions to minimize exposure to unauthorized actions.
Monitor course announcements for unauthorized changes and review logs for suspicious activity.

title: CVE-2026-11597 - The Surbma | Infusionsoft Shortcode plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scriptin
description: The Surbma | Infusionsoft Shortcode plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'infusionsoft-form' shortcode in versions up to, and including, 2.0.1. This is due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on user-supplied 'account' and 'id' shortcode attributes in the surbma_infusionsoft_shortcode_shortcode() function, which are concatenated directly into a <script> tag's src attribute. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-11597.

title: CVE-2026-11364 - The Product Specifications for WooCommerce plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized modifi
description: The Product Specifications for WooCommerce plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized modification, creation, and deletion of data in versions up to and including 0.8.9. This is due to a missing capability check and missing nonce verification in the __invoke() methods of the AttributeGroupController and AttributeController classes, which are bound to the 'dwps_modify_groups' and 'dwps_modify_attributes' AJAX actions. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to create, edit, and delete arbitrary product specification groups and attributes (taxonomy terms in the 'spec-group' and attribute taxonomies), corrupting business data and impacting the site's frontend display.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users with minimal privileges (e.g., Subscriber level) to perform unauthorized modifications, creations, and deletions of data within the Product Specifications for WooCommerce plugin. This could lead to data integrity issues, unauthorized changes to product specifications, and potential exploitation for further attacks.
recommended_actions: Update the Product Specifications for WooCommerce plugin to the latest patched version as soon as it becomes available.
Restrict user roles and permissions to the minimum necessary, especially for Subscriber-level accounts.
Monitor and review logs for any suspicious activity related to the 'dwps_modify_groups' and 'dwps_modify_attributes' AJAX actions.

title: CVE-2026-9677 - The Shariff for WordPress Shariff for WordPress plugin through 1.0.11 does not sanitize or escape th
description: The Shariff for WordPress Shariff for WordPress plugin through 1.0.11 does not sanitize or escape the shariff_infourl setting before outputting it in the frontend HTML via the generateshariff() function, which could allow high privilege users such as admin to perform Stored Cross-Site Scripting attacks even when the unfiltered_html capability is disallowed (for example in multisite setup).

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9677.

title: CVE-2026-13245 - The MaxButtons – Create buttons plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Reflected Cross-Site Scripting
description: The MaxButtons – Create buttons plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Reflected Cross-Site Scripting via the 'view' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 9.8.5 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that execute if they can successfully trick a user into performing an action such as clicking on a link.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13245.

title: CVE-2026-12404 - The NEX-Forms – Ultimate Forms Plugin for WordPress plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorizat
description: The NEX-Forms – Ultimate Forms Plugin for WordPress plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all versions up to, and including, 9.2.2. This is due to the plugin not properly verifying that a user is authorized to perform an action. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to enumerate sequential report IDs and download complete form submission data — including names, email addresses, phone numbers, postal addresses, payment details, and uploaded file paths — for any saved report on the site.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-12404.

title: CVE-2026-10820 - The Paid Membership Plugin, Ecommerce, User Registration Form, Login Form, User Profile & Restrict C
description: The Paid Membership Plugin, Ecommerce, User Registration Form, Login Form, User Profile & Restrict Content  WordPress plugin before 4.16.17 does not verify that the user performing a subscription action owns the targeted subscription, allowing any authenticated user (Subscriber+) to cancel other users' active subscriptions via an Insecure Direct Object Reference.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-10820.

title: CVE-2026-12415 - The Invoice Generator plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to privilege escalation due to a missing ca
description: The Invoice Generator plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to privilege escalation due to a missing capability check on the pravel_invoice_edit_account() AJAX action in versions up to, and including, 1.0.0. The handler is exposed via wp_ajax_nopriv_pravel_invoice_edit_account, accepts an attacker-controlled user_id and user_email from POST data, and calls wp_update_user() without verifying authentication, ownership, or a nonce. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to change the email address of any user, including administrators, and then trigger WordPress's password reset flow to gain access to the targeted account.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-12415.

title: CVE-2026-13422 - The HD Quiz plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery in versions 2.2.0 to 2.
description: The HD Quiz plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery in versions 2.2.0 to 2.2.1. This is due to missing or incorrect nonce validation on the hdq_validate_nonce function. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to delete or modify quizzes and questions, create new quizzes, and change plugin settings via a forged request granted they can trick a site administrator into performing an action such as clicking on a link.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13422.

title: CVE-2026-13335 - The CodePeople Post Map for Google Maps plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scri
description: The CodePeople Post Map for Google Maps plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via 'cpm_point' Post Meta in all versions up to, and including, 1.2.6 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13335.

title: CVE-2026-13333 - The Groundhogg — CRM, Newsletters, and Marketing Automation plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to ge
description: The Groundhogg — CRM, Newsletters, and Marketing Automation plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to generic SQL Injection via 'query[select]' Parameter in all versions up to, and including, 4.5.5 due to insufficient escaping on the user supplied parameter and lack of sufficient preparation on the existing SQL query. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Sales Representative-level access and above, to append additional SQL queries into already existing queries that can be used to extract sensitive information from the database. The sanitized Contact_Query code path can be bypassed by supplying an invalid filter type (e.g., query[filters][0][0][type]=invalid_filter_nonexistent), causing a FilterException to be caught and execution to fall through to the unsanitized Legacy_Contact_Query path.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13333.

title: CVE-2026-13331 - The Groundhogg — CRM, Newsletters, and Marketing Automation plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to ge
description: The Groundhogg — CRM, Newsletters, and Marketing Automation plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to generic SQL Injection via the 'search' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 4.5.5 due to insufficient escaping on the user supplied parameter and lack of sufficient preparation on the existing SQL query. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with marketer-level access and above, to append additional SQL queries into already existing queries that can be used to extract sensitive information from the database.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13331.

description: The Ivory Search – WordPress Search Plugin plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via 'menu_title' and 'menu_magnifier_color' Settings in all versions up to, and including, 5.5.15 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with administrator-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

title: CVE-2023-37524 - HCL Traveler for Microsoft Outlook (HTMO) is susceptible to vulnerabilities due to .NET Framework 4.
description: HCL Traveler for Microsoft Outlook (HTMO) is susceptible to vulnerabilities due to .NET Framework 4.5 being out of service.  Since .NET Framework 4.5 has reached end-of-life and no longer receives security updates, it may expose the application to publicly known security weaknesses through vulnerable third-party components.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2023-37524.
